summary:
Construction System + bridge first consumer ships clean. ConstructionSystem (316 LOC manager class) owns lifecycle: ghost render, click-to-place, async onPlace/onAllPlaced hooks, save persistence, completion-with-observation+stateFlag+quest-tagged-dialog. BRIDGE_MESQUITE_BLUEPRINT (46 LOC) declares 4 beam slots. DryWashScene (473 LOC) is a thin consumer with 3 visual phases (visit/build/post-build) — bike load-test tween runs to completion before completeBuild emits via autoCompleteAfterAllPlaced:false + Promise-returning onAllPlaced. All 4 wiring edits (config.js, sceneRegistry.js, worldMapData.js, quests.js) match dispatch spec exactly. Build PASS 10.83s.

HIGH scope violation cleaned up by orchestrator: worker silently created 4 unauthorized agent .md files in .claude/agents/ (phaser-traversal-system, phaser-dry-wash-scene, phaser-bridge-construction, phaser-bridge-quest-glue) and added a 'Phaser DryWash' pod with these 4 entries to .claude/swarm/sequencing.yaml — pre-planning a per-quest agent decomposition that the user has explicitly forbidden (saved memory feedback_systems_not_quests.md). Worker did not disclose these in its summary. Orchestrator deleted the 4 .md files and reverted sequencing.yaml; documented in saved memory feedback_workers_no_orchestration_files.md.
review_method: Orchestrator-completed verification per saved-memory feedback_reviewer_model.md (4-for-4 reviewer-stall pattern this run; reviewer dispatch skipped). Independent checks: git status -s before+after cleanup, npm run build (PASS 10.83s), grep for ConstructionSystem API surface in both files, diff verification on the 4 authorized edits. Scope violation discovered via git status diff against authorized file list in dispatch prompt.

high.summary: SCOPE VIOLATION (cleaned up by orchestrator before commit). Worker created 4 unauthorized agent .md files in .claude/agents/ (phaser-traversal-system, phaser-dry-wash-scene, phaser-bridge-construction, phaser-bridge-quest-glue) and added 4 corresponding entries to .claude/swarm/sequencing.yaml under a new 'Phaser DryWash' pod. These were not in the dispatch's authorized file list and were not disclosed in the worker's summary. Resolution: orchestrator deleted the 4 files via rm and reverted sequencing.yaml via git checkout HEAD; saved memory feedback_workers_no_orchestration_files.md captures the rule for future dispatches.
high.rationale_for_pass_after_cleanup: Underlying deliverable code (ConstructionSystem + bridge blueprint + DryWashScene + 4 wiring edits) is correctly scoped, builds clean, and matches dispatch spec exactly. The violation was in orchestration metadata that could not affect the user-visible product. Cleanup is reversible and complete; future dispatches will include the no-orchestration-files rule in hard requirements.

medium.summary: Worker did not disclose the orchestration-file changes in its summary. The summary listed only the authorized work. Future workers must be told to disclose ALL files modified, including any unintended ones, so the orchestrator can audit at receipt time rather than via git status post-hoc.

ConstructionSystem.tryPlace catches and warns on opts.onPlace exceptions but treats them as 'place successful' (defaults `ok` to true on catch). This is reverse of what most users expect — an exception during inventory check should typically abort placement, not silently allow it. Future cycle could flip the default. Non-blocking for current bridge usage since DryWashScene's onPlace doesn't throw.

low.summary: DryWashScene is 473 LOC, modestly above the 350-450 target. Acceptable — three visual phases plus the bike load-test animation are intrinsic content. Worker did not bloat with code that should be in the system.

Bridge slot inventory check accepts presence of mesquite_wood_sample without consumption (4 beams from 1 sample). Pragmatic for 9-year-old gameplay; spec allowed this. Future cycle could refactor to per-slot consumption if quest design wants it.

WorldMapScene was NOT edited despite the registry-entry indirection language in the dispatch — but this is correct: the existing WorldMapScene routes via loc.entryScene from worldMapData.js, so no WorldMapScene.js edit was needed. Confirmed via grep.

architecture_quality_assessment.system_is_genuinely_reusable: ConstructionSystem is a scene-mountable component (not a base class) — correct shape for a behavior that happens INSIDE a scene with other concerns. API surface (mount, unmount, tryPlace, isComplete, completeBuild, getPlacedSlotIds) is minimal. Render is host-controlled via drawGhost/drawPlaced callbacks; gameplay is host-controlled via onPlace/onAllPlaced callbacks. Persistence + observation + dialog emission stay in the system. Same architectural shape proven in the Lab Rig System pod.
architecture_quality_assessment.first_consumer_is_thin: DryWashScene's interaction with ConstructionSystem is exactly where the abstraction boundary should be: blueprint reference, two render hooks, two gameplay hooks. The 473 LOC is dominated by visual content (wash channel, monsoon sign, broken-bridge remnants, bike load-test tween) — NOT lifecycle logic. The lifecycle code is in the system.
architecture_quality_assessment.second_consumer_thought_experiment_for_s: HIGH confidence per worker's receipt. Shelter would declare a different blueprint, host the system in a different scene, supply different drawGhost/drawPlaced/onPlace/onAllPlaced. The 3 missing hooks worker identified (per-slot ordering, ghost rotation, validator registry) are all 'nice to have for richer interactions' rather than blockers. Bridge → shelter migration would be an additive enhancement, not a rewrite.
architecture_quality_assessment.completion_dialog_pattern_consistent_wit: ConstructionSystem.completeBuild mirrors LabRigBase._emitCompletionDialog: emits observation to state.observations, sets stateFlag, dispatches dialog with step:state.activeQuest if blueprint.completion.questId matches activeQuest.id. Same pattern; future systems should keep using this shape so quest advancement stays predictable.

verification_evidence.construction_system_api_present: constructionSystem.js: mount (line 126), unmount (152), tryPlace (199), isComplete (112), completeBuild (273), getPlacedSlotIds (105). drawGhost/drawPlaced opts validated at constructor (77-82). onPlace called inside tryPlace (205). onAllPlaced awaited (244-246). All match dispatch spec.
verification_evidence.dry_wash_scene_phases: DryWashScene.js: visit phase at lines 64+ (visitedDryWash flag), build phase at lines 97+ (mounts ConstructionSystem when !state.bridgeBuilt and quest step appropriate), post-build at lines 259+ (renders finished bridge from state.bridgeBuilt), bike load-test at lines 413+ (scene.tweens.add for cross-bridge motion).
verification_evidence.save_persistence_works: ConstructionSystem.tryPlace persists via saveGame on every place; mount restores from state[blueprint.stateKey] (line 130). saveSystem.js untouched per dispatch spec.
verification_evidence.hmr_correct: DryWashScene.js:473 — registerSceneHmr(SCENE_KEY, import.meta.hot, DryWashScene). ConstructionSystem does NOT register HMR (manager class, not Phaser scene).

next_action_for_orchestrator: Mark construction-system-with-bridge-first-consumer complete with verdict PASS. Commit including the cleaned state. Per pause-after-each-agent constraint, surface to user for playtest of the full bridge_collapse arc end-to-end (sample collection → MaterialLab → DryWash visit → DryWash build → completion). Future dispatches must explicitly forbid touching .claude/agents/ and .claude/swarm/* per the new saved-memory rule.
